children.heading: Occupational Safety and Health Protections.—
children.label: a Occupational Safety and Health Protections.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Each employing office and each covered employee shall comply with the provisions of section 5 of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “employer” as used in such Act means an employing office; and

children.label: B
children.text: the term “employee” as used in such Act means a covered employee.

children.heading: .—Remedy
children.label: b .—Remedy
children.text: The remedy for a violation of subsection (a) shall be an order to correct the violation, including such order as would be appropriate if issued under section 13(a) of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970.

children.heading: .—Requests for inspections
children.label: 1 .—Requests for inspections
children.text: Upon written request of any employing office or covered employee, the Secretary of Labor shall have the authority to inspect and investigate places of employment under the jurisdiction of employing offices in accordance with subsections (a), (d), (e), and (f) of section 8 of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970.

children.label: A
children.text: a citation or notice to any employing office responsible for correcting a violation of subsection (a); or

children.label: B
children.text: a notification to any employing office that the Secretary of Labor believes has failed to correct a violation for which a citation has been issued within the period permitted for its correction.

children.label: A
children.text: the citation and notification shall be deemed a final order (within the meaning of section 10(b) of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970) if the employer fails to notify the Secretary of Labor within 15 days (excluding Saturdays, Sundays, and Federal holidays) after receipt of the notice that the employer intends to contest the citation or notification; or

children.label: B
children.text: opportunity for a hearing before the Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission shall be afforded in accordance with section 10(c) of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970, if the employer gives timely notice to the Secretary that he intends to contest the citation or notification.

children.heading: .—Variance procedures
children.label: 4 .—Variance procedures
children.text: An employing office may request from the Secretary of Labor an order granting a variance from a standard made applicable by this section, in accordance with sections 6(b)(6) and 6(d) of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970.

children.heading: .—Judicial review
children.label: 5 .—Judicial review
children.text: Any person or employing office aggrieved by a final decision of the Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission under paragraph (3) or the Secretary of Labor under paragraph (4) may file a petition for review with the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit under .section 1296 of title 28

children.heading: .—Compliance date
children.label: 6 .—Compliance date
children.text: If new appropriated funds are necessary to correct a violation of subsection (a) for which a citation is issued, or to comply with an order requiring correction of such a violation, correction or compliance shall take place as soon as possible, but not later than the end of the fiscal year following the fiscal year in which the citation is issued or the order requiring correction becomes final and not subject to further review.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The President, or the designee of the President, shall issue regulations to implement this section.

children.label: A
children.text: except to the extent that the President or designee may determine, for good cause shown and stated together with the regulation, that a modification of such regulations would be more effective for the implementation of the rights and protections under this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: would be equally effective for the implementation of the rights and protections under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: would promote uniformity in the application of Federal law to employees in the executive branch of the Federal Government.

children.heading: .—Employing office responsible for correction
children.label: 3 .—Employing office responsible for correction
children.text: The regulations issued under paragraph (1) shall include a method of identifying, for purposes of this section and for different categories of violations of subsection (a), the employing office responsible for correction of a particular violation.
the effective date of regulations issued under subsection (d); or
